meta.title: Performance & Test Methodology
meta.description: How Taypro defines, tests, and reports 99%+ dust-removal claims, soiling-related generation recovery, and platform-specific performance for solar cleaning robots in India.

purpose.heading: Purpose
purpose.body: This page explains how Taypro Private Limited defines, measures, and communicates performance figures on taypro.in, including "99%+" dust-removal statements and references to generation or performance ratio recovery. It is intended for asset owners, EPC teams, and O&M leads evaluating robotic dry cleaning for utility-scale PV in India.

dustRemoval.heading: What "99%+ dust removal" means
dustRemoval.li0: Dust removal is the fraction of loose surface particulate removed from the module glass in one completed cleaning pass (HELYX) or automated dual-pass cycle (GLYDE, GLYDE-X), under defined test or field conditions.
dustRemoval.li1: It does not mean the solar panel converts 99% of sunlight to electricity, nor that the plant will gain 99% more energy output.
dustRemoval.li2: Figures are platform-dependent: brush-based semi-automatic paths (HELYX) and dual-pass autonomous paths (GLYDE / GLYDE-X) use different mechanics and are quoted per pass or per cycle accordingly.

generation.heading: Generation recovery vs. module efficiency
generation.p0: Soiling on Indian utility plants can suppress usable output when washing is infrequent, industry discussions often cite roughly 8–25% seasonal soiling loss depending on region, tilt, and O&M practice. Robotic dry cleaning aims to recover energy that would otherwise be lost to dust, not to raise nameplate module efficiency.
generation.p1Before: Actual MWh recovered depends on your baseline soiling, cleaning cadence, tariff, and weather. Taypro does not publish a single universal "efficiency increase %" for all sites. Use the
generation.p1After: for directional economics, then request a plant-specific assessment from our applications team.

testing.heading: How we test and validate
testing.intro: Taypro validates cleaning performance through a combination of:
testing.li0: Controlled bench and field trials on representative module types, using standardised artificial and natural dust loads where applicable.
testing.li1: Visual and gravimetric checks (before/after surface condition and mass of removed dust) on deployed platforms.
testing.li2: Operational data from live fleets, cycle completion, route coverage, and performance ratio trends where customers share SCADA or IV data under NDA.
testing.li3: Third-party review, selected platforms are TÜV NORD certified; certification scope is stated on product datasheets and proposals.
testing.note: Results vary with dust type (agricultural film, sand, pollen, post-storm mix), module tilt, tracker motion, and whether one or two passes are completed in a cycle.

platform.heading: Platform summary
platform.glydeMethod: Airflow + microfiber drum; autonomous row run
platform.helyxMethod: Counter-rotating PBT brushes; operator placement per row
platform.glydeXMetric: 99%+ dust per dual-pass cycle on tracker tables
platform.glydeXMethod: Dual-pass dry cleaning with flexible bridge; tracker compatibility per site assessment

uptime.heading: Uptime and service commitments
uptime.body: Marketing references to "highest uptime" or "same-day breakdown" response describe Taypro service targets backed by pan-India spares, NECTYR remote diagnostics, and AMC programs. Contractual SLAs are defined in customer agreements, not on this page.

publicProof.heading: Fleet impact figures on taypro.in
publicProof.body: Hero and company stats such as 5 GW+ capacity, 11 billion+ panels cleaned annually (11,00,00,00,000 panel passes per year, fleet aggregate), 150+ live sites, 188 GWh+ additional clean generation, 700M+ litres of water saved annually, and 93k+ metric tons of CO₂ avoided use approved public marketing assumptions for India utility-scale fleets. The annual panel-cleaning figure aggregates completed cleaning passes across deployed automatic and semi-automatic fleets. Water-savings estimates follow CEEW-class wet-wash benchmarks (~7,000 L per MW per cycle × ~20 cycles per year vs. waterless robotic cleaning). Generation recovery figures compare waterless robotic cadence to typical wet-wash baselines, not nameplate module efficiency. Directional ESG model outputs may differ slightly from rounded public tiles; plant-specific outcomes require site assessment.

documentation.heading: Plant-specific documentation
documentation.bodyBefore: For tenders, lender diligence, or internal technical review, Taypro can provide site-specific cleaning plans, cycle assumptions, and, where available, anonymised field outcomes from comparable deployments.
documentation.bodyAfter: with plant capacity, layout, and location.
